summary: A 2D 1v1 artillery battle game where the player places units inside a mobile castle and aims their weapons to destroy the opponent's castle before their own is destroyed.
objective: Reduce the enemy (Red) castle's health to 0% by firing projectiles from units placed inside the player's (Blue) castle.

controls.observation: Drag and drop a unit card from the bottom UI onto a valid platform inside the castle to place the unit.
Drag from a placed unit to aim its weapon. A dotted trajectory line appears. Release to fire.

layout.observation: Top UI: Blue vs Red health bars with percentage text and castle icons.
Bottom UI: Three unit selection cards (Cyclops, Skeleton, Orc) appear when zoomed into the player's castle.
End screen with game logo 'Castle Clashers' and a 'PLAY' button.

entities.name: Blue Castle
entities.visible_behavior: Takes damage, visually degrades, houses player units on internal platforms.

entities.name: Red Castle
entities.visible_behavior: Takes damage, visually degrades, automatically fires projectiles at the player.

entities.name: Cyclops Unit
entities.visible_behavior: Placed on platforms, fires a single rocket projectile when aimed.

entities.name: Skeleton Unit
entities.visible_behavior: Placed on platforms, fires a barrage of multiple rockets when aimed.

entities.name: Orc Unit
entities.visible_behavior: Placed on platforms, fires an explosive projectile.

entities.name: Rocket Projectile
entities.visible_behavior: Travels along a trajectory, leaves a smoke trail, explodes on impact dealing damage.

timeline.observation: Game starts. Both castles at 100% health. Red castle fires immediately.
Blue castle hit, health drops to 67%. Visual damage appears.
Player drags Cyclops unit from UI to a platform.
Player aims and fires Cyclops weapon. Rocket hits Red castle, health drops to 56%.
Red castle fires back, hitting Blue castle. Blue health drops to 35%.
Player places Skeleton unit and fires a barrage of rockets.
Rockets hit Red castle, health drops to 44%.
Red castle fires back, hitting Blue castle. Blue health drops to 31%.
Player places Orc unit and fires.
Red castle fires back, hitting Blue castle. Blue health drops to 17%.
Player re-aims the Skeleton unit, firing rockets upwards.
Rockets rain down on Red castle, health drops to 30%.

mechanics.observation: Castles have a global health pool represented by a percentage.
Units can only be placed on designated horizontal platforms inside the player's castle.
Aiming uses a drag-to-aim mechanic that calculates and displays a ballistic trajectory.
Projectiles deal damage upon collision with the enemy castle's collision bounds.
The enemy AI automatically fires projectiles at intervals.

visual_requirements: Parallax scrolling background with multiple layers (sky, mountains, trees).
Dynamic castle destruction visuals (chunks of stone flying off, smoke, fire, revealing interior structure).
Explosion VFX and screen shake on impact.
Dotted line trajectory indicator during aiming.
prototype_must_have: Ability to drag and drop at least one unit type onto a platform.
Firing projectiles that apply physics and deal damage on collision.
Basic enemy AI that fires back periodically.
prototype_can_skip: Complex visual destruction of the castles (simple health bar reduction is fine).
Multiple unit types (one is sufficient for core loop).
implementation_notes: Use a 2D physics engine for projectile trajectories and collisions.
The trajectory line can be rendered using a LineRenderer or a series of sprite dots calculated via standard kinematic equations.
Castle damage can be managed by a single health script attached to the castle root, with colliders covering the visual bounds.
Unit placement requires defining valid drop zones (colliders or specific transforms) inside the castle.

uncertainties.question: Do units have individual health or cooldowns?
uncertainties.why_it_matters: Affects game balance and whether the player needs to constantly replace units or just wait to fire them again.

uncertainties.question: Is the enemy AI aiming dynamically or firing at fixed targets?
uncertainties.why_it_matters: Changes the complexity of the AI implementation.

uncertainties.question: What determines the damage amount of a projectile?
uncertainties.why_it_matters: Needed for accurate combat math. It might be fixed per unit type or randomized.
